exercises.example_sentence: 午後三時ごろに会いましょう。
wrong_choice_explanations: まで means 'until' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about an approximate meeting time, not an endpoint.
から means 'from/since' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when to meet, not a starting point.
ちょうど means 'exactly' — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time, not an exact time.
exercises.hiragana_full: ごごさんじごろにあいましょう。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 午後三時ごろに会おう。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ごごさんじごろにあおう。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 午後三時ごろ会いましょう。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ごごさんじごろあいましょう。

exercises.translation: I go to bed around 10 PM.
wrong_choice_explanations: に indicates an exact time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time.
で indicates location or means — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about time, not place or method.
まで means 'until' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when we go to bed, not staying up until that time.
exercises.hiragana_full: よるのじゅうじごろねます

audio_alternatives.kanji: 夜十時ごろに寝ます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: よるじゅうじごろにねます

audio_alternatives.kanji: 夜の十時ごろ寝る。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: よるのじゅうじごろねる

exercises.example_sentence: 朝の七時ごろ起きました。
wrong_choice_explanations: まえに means 'before' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about the approximate time of waking up, not before it.
あとで means 'after/later' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about the time of waking up, not something that happens later.
から means 'from' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when we woke up, not starting from that time.
exercises.hiragana_full: あさのしちじごろおきました

audio_alternatives.kanji: 朝の七時ごろ目が覚めました。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あさのしちじごろめがさめました

audio_alternatives.kanji: 午前七時ごろ起きました。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ごぜんしちじごろおきました

wrong_choice_explanations: に indicates a specific time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time period.
で indicates location — it doesn't fit here because spring is a time period, not a place.
には indicates a specific time or condition — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time period.
exercises.hiragana_full: はるごろさくらがさきます

audio_alternatives.kanji: 春ごろに桜が咲きます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: はるごろにさくらがさきます

audio_alternatives.kanji: 春ごろ桜が咲く。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: はるごろさくらがさく

exercises.example_sentence: お昼の十二時ごろお弁当を食べます。
exercises.translation: I eat my lunch box around 12 PM.
wrong_choice_explanations: ちょうど means 'exactly' — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time, not an exact time.
すぎに means 'past/after' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about the approximate time of eating, not after that time.
までに means 'by (deadline)' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when we eat, not a deadline to finish eating.
exercises.hiragana_full: おひるのじゅうにじごろおべんとうをたべます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 十二時ごろ昼ご飯を食べます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: じゅうにじごろひるごはんをたべます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: お昼ごろお弁当を食べます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: おひるごろおべんとうをたべます。

wrong_choice_explanations: には indicates a specific time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time period.
だけ means 'only' — it doesn't fit here because we're not limiting to winter exclusively, just indicating the approximate time.
まで means 'until' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when it snows, not a duration ending in winter.
exercises.hiragana_full: ふゆごろゆきがふります

audio_alternatives.kanji: 冬ごろ雪が降る。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ふゆごろゆきがふる

audio_alternatives.kanji: 冬に雪が降ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ふゆにゆきがふります

exercises.example_sentence: 夕方の六時ごろ帰ります。
wrong_choice_explanations: までに means 'by (deadline)' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about approximate departure time, not a deadline.
すぎに means 'past/after' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about the approximate time of leaving, not after 6 PM.
ちょうど means 'exactly' — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time, not an exact time.
exercises.hiragana_full: ゆうがたのろくじごろかえります

audio_alternatives.kanji: 午後六時ごろ帰ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ごごろくじごろかえります

audio_alternatives.kanji: 夕方の六時ごろ家に帰ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ゆうがたのろくじごろいえにかえります

exercises.example_sentence: 授業は九時ごろ始まります。
wrong_choice_explanations: に indicates an exact time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate starting time.
から means 'from' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when class starts, not the duration from that time.
まで means 'until' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when class starts, not when it ends.
exercises.hiragana_full: じゅぎょうはくじごろはじまります

audio_alternatives.kanji: 授業は九時ごろ始まる。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: じゅぎょうはくじごろはじまる

audio_alternatives.kanji: クラスは九時ごろ始まります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: くらすはくじごろはじまります

exercises.example_sentence: 秋ごろ紅葉が美しいです。
exercises.translation: The autumn leaves are beautiful around fall.
wrong_choice_explanations: には indicates a specific time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time period when leaves are beautiful.
で indicates location — it doesn't fit here because fall is a time period, not a place.
まで means 'until' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about when leaves are beautiful, not a duration ending in fall.
exercises.hiragana_full: あきごろこうようがうつくしいです

audio_alternatives.kanji: 秋ごろ紅葉がきれいです。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あきごろこうようがきれいです

audio_alternatives.kanji: 秋に紅葉が美しいです。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あきにこうようがうつくしいです

exercises.example_sentence: 夜中の二時ごろ電話がかかってきました。
exercises.translation: I received a phone call around 2 AM.
wrong_choice_explanations: ちょうど means 'exactly' — it doesn't fit here because we want to express approximate time, not exact time.
に indicates an exact time — it doesn't fit here because we want to express an approximate time when the call came.
すぎに means 'past/after' — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about the approximate time the call came, not after 2 AM.

audio_alternatives.kanji: 夜中の二時ごろ電話がありました。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: よなかのにじごろでんわがありました

audio_alternatives.kanji: 深夜二時ごろに電話がかかってきました。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: しんやにじごろにでんわがかかってきました
